title: Uplifting Dutch Rice Stew
description: A fermented, pillowy rice stew with garam masala and Mediterranean flair served from an agave leaf.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: or low-sodium vegetable broth for extra flavor

ingredients.name: fermented coconut milk
ingredients.notes: prepared by fermenting coconut milk with a starter culture

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and carrot and sauté until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the rice and stir to coat the grains in oil and spices, then add garam masala, cumin, smoked paprika, and turmeric, cooking for another minute.
Pour in the water and bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
After 15 minutes, add the fermented coconut milk, stir gently, and continue simmering for another 10 minutes until the rice is tender and the mixture has thickened.
Season with salt to taste and stir in chopped cilantro if desired.
Remove from heat, fluff the rice with a fork, and let it sit covered for 5 minutes to become pillowy.
Serve the stew warm, optionally topped with a drizzle of olive oil or extra fresh herbs.
